title_en: How to Get the Perfect Fade — And Keep It
title_el: Πώς πετυχαίνεις το τέλειο fade — και πώς το κρατάς
excerpt_en: A clean fade is half about the cut and half about the routine. Here's what we ask of every client who wants one to last.
excerpt_el: Ένα καθαρό fade είναι μισό δουλειά του μπαρμπέρη και μισό ρουτίνα στο σπίτι. Να τι ζητάμε από κάθε πελάτη που θέλει να κρατήσει.
body_en:
The perfect fade starts with a conversation. Before any clipper touches the head, we talk about bone structure, hair density, and lifestyle. A skin fade on a desk worker looks different from one on a stonemason, and we build for both.

The cut itself

We work from a 0.5 up to a number 3, blending in half-guard increments. The key is not the numbers — it's the transitions. Good fades disappear between steps.

What to do at home

  • Book the follow-up before you leave. Two to three weeks is the window.
  • Use a light tonic, not a heavy pomade, around the temples. Heavy product collects in the short hair and breaks the blend.
  • Brush dry, never wet. Wet brushing lifts the cuticle and blurs the line.

Come in every three weeks for a clean-up. The cut will tell you when it's ready.
body_el:
Το τέλειο fade ξεκινά με μια κουβέντα. Πριν πιάσουμε ψαλίδι, μιλάμε για το σχήμα του κρανίου, την πυκνότητα του μαλλιού και τη ζωή του πελάτη.

Η κοπή

Δουλεύουμε από 0.5 μέχρι νούμερο 3, σπάζοντας με μισά βήματα. Το μυστικό δεν είναι τα νούμερα — είναι οι μεταβάσεις.

Τι κάνεις στο σπίτι

  • Κλείσε το επόμενο ραντεβού πριν φύγεις.
  • Χρησιμοποίησε ελαφρύ tonic, όχι βαριά πομάδα, στους κροτάφους.
  • Βούρτσισε σε στεγνό μαλλί, ποτέ σε βρεγμένο.

title_en: The Five-Minute Morning Routine for a Sharper Look
title_el: Η πρωινή ρουτίνα των πέντε λεπτών
excerpt_en: You don't need twelve products to look put-together. Here's the short version — cleanser, tonic, comb, pomade, done.
excerpt_el: Δεν χρειάζεσαι δώδεκα προϊόντα. Τέσσερα, σωστά, σε βγάζουν έξω κάθε πρωί σε πέντε λεπτά.
body_en:
Most men we see have one of two problems: they use too much product, or they skip the basics. The routine below takes five minutes and covers both.

1 — Cleanse, don't strip

Sulfate-free shampoo every other day, warm water. Daily washing dries the scalp and triggers more oil.

2 — Tonic while damp

A light tonic on damp hair sets the base. It refreshes the scalp and gives pomade something to grip.

3 — Comb, then hands

Comb the shape, then break it up with your fingers for a natural finish. A comb alone reads too formal.

4 — Pomade sparingly

Pea-sized amount, warm it in your palms first. Work from the back forward — never from the front down.
body_el: Οι περισσότεροι άντρες έχουν ένα από δύο προβλήματα: πολύ προϊόν ή μηδέν βάση. Η ρουτίνα από κάτω λύνει και τα δύο.

title_en: Beard Oil or Balm? Here's the Honest Answer
title_el: Λάδι ή balm για το μούσι; Η ειλικρινής απάντηση
excerpt_en: They do different jobs. Most men need both — and most men use neither. A three-minute read to fix that.
excerpt_el: Κάνουν διαφορετική δουλειά. Οι περισσότεροι άντρες χρειάζονται και τα δύο — και δεν χρησιμοποιούν κανένα.
body_en:
The short answer: oil for the skin underneath, balm for the hair on top.

Beard oil

Argan and jojoba feed the skin and stop the itchy-week that kills most beard attempts around day five. Three drops in the palm, rub, press into the skin under the beard. Not on top of the hair.

Beard balm

Shea butter and beeswax. This is your styling tool — it tames wild hairs, gives a light hold, and protects against wind and cold.

The order

Oil first, on clean damp skin. Let it settle for a minute. Then balm on dry hair. If you want to add anything, it's a wide-tooth comb.

How much?

Less than you think. A beard past your collar still only needs a pea-sized amount of balm. Overdoing it looks wet, feels greasy, and attracts dust.
body_el: Σύντομη απάντηση: το λάδι για το δέρμα από κάτω, το balm για την τρίχα από πάνω.

title_en: How Often Should You Actually Wash Your Hair?
excerpt_en: Not every day. Not once a week. The answer depends on your hair type — and what you're putting in it.
excerpt_el: Ούτε κάθε μέρα, ούτε μία φορά τη βδομάδα. Η απάντηση εξαρτάται από τον τύπο του μαλλιού σου.
body_en:
Daily shampoo is the single most common reason we see dull, brittle hair. The scalp needs its natural oils — strip them every day and it fights back by producing more.

Fine, straight hair

Every 2 days. Use a sulfate-free formula and alternate with water-only rinses.

Thick or wavy

Every 3 days is plenty. Conditioner only on the mid-lengths and ends — never on the scalp.

Heavy product users

If you use pomade or clay, you need to clarify once a week with a stronger wash. Water alone won't break waxes down.

Water temperature matters

Warm to open the cuticle, cold at the end to close it. Thirty seconds of cold makes a visible difference in shine.
body_el: Το καθημερινό λούσιμο είναι ο πρώτος λόγος που βλέπουμε μπερδεμένα, άτονα μαλλιά. Το τριχωτό χρειάζεται τα φυσικά του έλαια.

title_en: Behind the Chair — A Morning with Andreas
title_el: Στην καρέκλα — ένα πρωινό με τον Ανδρέα
excerpt_en: Fifteen years, three chairs, one straight razor. A short interview with our master barber about the craft.
excerpt_el: Δεκαπέντε χρόνια, τρεις καρέκλες, ένα ξυράφι. Μια κουβέντα με τον master barber μας.
body_en:
We caught up with Andreas between appointments. He started cutting at seventeen, apprenticed in Athens, and has been at Oakline since day one.

On the straight razor

"The razor is honest. It tells you if your technique is good — there's no second chance. That's why I still use it every day, even when clippers would be faster."

On regulars

"You learn a person's hair over time. After the third or fourth visit, you know before they sit down what they want. That relationship is the whole job, really. The cut is almost a side effect."

On what's changed

"Men care more now. Fifteen years ago, a haircut was a chore. Now it's something guys look forward to. That change is the best thing that's happened to the industry."
body_el: Μιλήσαμε με τον Ανδρέα ανάμεσα στα ραντεβού. Κουρεύει από τα δεκαεφτά του και είναι στο Oakline από την πρώτη μέρα.

title_en: New Hours — We're Open Later on Saturdays
title_el: Νέες ώρες — Ανοιχτά αργότερα το Σάββατο
excerpt_en: Starting next month, Saturday service runs until 8pm. Same barbers, same booking flow, more slots.
excerpt_el: Από τον επόμενο μήνα, το Σάββατο μένουμε ανοιχτοί μέχρι τις 8 το βράδυ. Ίδιοι μπαρμπέρηδες, περισσότερα διαθέσιμα ραντεβού.
body_en:
Saturday has been our most-requested day for extended hours, and we listened. From May 1st, we're open until 20:00 on Saturdays, across all three chairs.

The online booking calendar already reflects the new hours — anything from 18:00 onwards on Saturday is now bookable. Walk-ins welcome too, though we'd recommend booking ahead during peak slots.

Other days

Tuesday through Friday stays at 09:00–21:00 as before. Sunday remains closed — some things are sacred.
body_el: Από την 1η Μαΐου, ανοιχτά μέχρι τις 20:00 κάθε Σάββατο. Οι νέες ώρες φαίνονται ήδη στο online ημερολόγιο.
